sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with a nonhealing foot ulcer on his right foot, which has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer has recently become red and is draining.
He reports no specific pain, but occasionally experiences throbbing.
The patient is concerned about the ulcer's healing and the redness and drainage.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, but his last A1c level is not well-controlled, reported to be around 10.
He has not been checking his sugar levels regularly.
He also mentions that his primary care physician (PCP) started him on antibiotics, but he has not yet picked them up from the pharmacy.
Review of systems: The patient mentions occasional throbbing pain in his foot.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, with no fever.
Physical exam: The physical exam reveals a one by two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
There is redness, drainage, edema, and fluid present.
The patient does not experience pain when the wound is touched, but reports increased pressure.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with an associated infection.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; infection; uncontrolled diabetes.
Plan:
I ordered an ABI (ankle-brachial index) to determine the blood supply in the foot and assess the potential for healing.
I performed a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice daily for seven days to treat the infection.
I also arranged for a surgical shoe to be provided to reduce pressure on the foot and promote healing.
I instructed the patient to return in two weeks for another debridement and follow-up.
Follow-up: The patient will return in two weeks for follow-up.
If the patient experiences any issues or concerns, they are advised to contact the office for an urgent appointment.
